author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
suramya, madhura⋅smita, sarva sallakṣaṇānvita,
balīyān, taruṇa, gambhīra
vāvadūka, priyabhāṣī, sudhī, sapratibhāśvāsī,
vidagdha, catura, sukhī, dhīra

kṛtajña, dakṣiṇa, preṣṭha, varīyān, kīrtimacchreṣṭha,
lalanā⋅mohana, kelipara
sunitya⋅nūtana⋅mūrti, kevala saundarya⋅sphūrti,
vaṁśī⋅gāne sudakṣa, tatpara

dhīrodātta, dhīraśānta, su⋅dhīralalita, kānta,
dhīroddhata, lalanānāyaka
ceṭaka⋅viṭa⋅veṣṭita, vidūṣaka⋅susevita,
pīṭhamarda, priya⋅narmasakha

e pañca sahāyayuta, nandīśvarapati⋅suta,
pati⋅upapati⋅bhāvācārī
anukūla, śaṭha, dhṛṣṭa, sadakṣiṇa, rasa-tṛṣṇa,
rasa-mūrti, nikuñjavihārī
en_translation:
Very charming, sweet-smiling, endowed with all good qualities, very powerful, youthful, and profound. Eloquent, sweet-speaking, wise, brilliantly confident, expert, clever, happy, and steadfast.

Grateful, obliging, most beloved, excellent, best among the renowned, enchanting to women, and absorbed in playful pastimes. Of an eternally new form, an embodiment of pure beauty, very expert and diligent in playing the flute.

He is a noble and self-controlled hero, a calm and peaceful hero, a gentle and playful hero, and an arrogant yet brave hero—the leader of women. Surrounded by attendants and jokers, well-served by clowns and close assistants, He is a dear playful friend.

Endowed with these five types of assistants, the son of Nanda Mahārāja behaves in the mood of both husband and paramour. He is a favorable hero, a cunning hero, an impudent hero, and an impartial hero; thirsty for rasa and the very embodiment of rasa, He sports in the groves.
